response_raw:
1. Rider objective & context
A single off-road motorcycle rider is traversing a multi-track dirt hillside area. The footage appears to be recorded from a drone or elevated static camera position, capturing the rider moving across and down a large open hill with multiple intersecting dirt tracks. The device timestamp reads 17:04 throughout all frames.

2. Rider
The rider is visible as a small dark figure on a motorcycle throughout the early-to-mid frames. Due to the significant viewing distance and small scale within the frame, no details about body position, gear, clothing colour beyond dark, or specific rider posture are discernible. The rider appears upright on the motorcycle in the frames where they are visible.

3. Terrain & environment
- Large open hillside with dry, sandy/clay-coloured soil
- Multiple intersecting dirt tracks are visible across the hillside, showing evidence of extensive prior use (numerous tyre ruts and worn lines)
- Scattered low shrubs and small bushes across the terrain
- Rocky/limestone outcroppings visible in the foreground of several frames
- A tree line visible along the ridgeline in the background
- The sky transitions across frames: partially cloudy to heavily overcast with dark storm clouds in later frames
- One frame (frame 14) shows only a dramatic stormy sky over a distant mountain range — a distinct shift in camera perspective
- The terrain appears dry overall

4. Events
- Frames 1–3: Rider is moving downhill toward the camera, on a central dirt track, transitioning through a track junction area
- Frames 4–9: Rider continues moving, progressively shifting to the left side of the frame; the camera or drone appears to pan/track and then reposition, causing the rider to move toward the upper-left of the frame
- Frames 10–11: Rider is visible as a very small figure near the upper-left area of the frame, appearing to continue descending or traversing the hillside
- Frames 12–13: Rider is still faintly visible on the far left; the camera view shifts significantly to show a wider perspective of the track network and hillside
- Frame 14: Camera view cuts to a completely different scene — a wide panoramic shot of a distant mountain range under heavy storm cloud cover; no rider visible
- Frame 15: Camera returns to the hillside terrain close-up, showing rocky ground and shrubs; no rider visible
- Frame 16: Different section of hillside showing grass and dirt tracks; no rider visible
- Frame 17: Wide view of a new section of the track network on open hillside; no rider visible

5. Outcome
The rider remains on the motorcycle and continues moving across the terrain throughout the frames in which they are visible. No crash, stop, or dismount is observed. In the later frames the rider is no longer visible, either having moved out of frame or the camera perspective having changed significantly. The sequence ends without the rider reappearing.

6. Unclear or not visible
- Rider's specific gear, helmet colour, or clothing details are not discernible due to viewing distance
- Motorcycle make, model, or colour cannot be determined at this distance
- Rider body position, foot placement, or hand position not visible
- Speed cannot be determined
- Whether the camera is drone-mounted or a fixed elevated position is not confirmed
- The reason for the dramatic camera cut to the storm/mountain panorama in frame 14 is not clear — it may represent a separate clip segment or camera reorientation
- Whether there are other riders or vehicles elsewhere on the track network cannot be confirmed; some small dark objects appear in a few frames but are too small and indistinct to identify
- The white circular UI elements visible in several frames (frames 4–11) appear to be on-screen interface elements from the playback app, not physical objects in the scene
